place_detail: Truro and Halifax NS (mostly rural)
place_notes: Nova Scotia only.
race_categories: Caucasian (88.5%); Latin American (3.8%); Aboriginal (7.7%)
race_crosswalk: Caucasian -> Not visible minority; Latin American -> Latin American; Aboriginal -> Indigenous (unspecified)
race_notes: Labeled 'Ethnicity'. Limitations acknowledge need for more ethnic diversity. Aboriginal 7.7% (n=2). CIHR-funded — breaks CIHR-no-race pattern.
education_detail: Parental education via Boyd-NP scale (not reported separately)
ses_indicators: Boyd-NP scale (parental education+income); household annual income
ses_detail: Boyd-NP mean 68.7 (SD 22.7). Household income $51-60K. Limitations acknowledge need for more income levels.
disability_notes: 30.8% comorbid learning disability.
indigenous_notes: Aboriginal 7.7% (n=2). Nova Scotia has significant Mi'kmaq population. No OCAP or Indigenous governance.
consort_equity_notes: Race, SES, sex, age reported. Limitations discuss diversity gaps. CIHR-funded trial that DOES report race.
confidence_notes: CIHR grant 158711. Second CIHR trial to report race (after #119). Aboriginal in NS (likely Mi'kmaq). Boyd-NP SES measure. Pediatric ADHD.

canadian_sites_named: Royal Ottawa Mental Health Centre, Ottawa ON
place_detail: Royal Ottawa, Ottawa ON
place_notes: Schizophrenia Outpatient Program.
race_notes: No race/ethnicity reported.
disability_notes: Learning disability and hearing impairment excluded.
consort_equity_notes: Only sex and age. Small crossover n=20.
confidence_notes: UMRF coded Foundation. 2F/18M. Smoking status reported. No registration.

intervention: Low-dose buprenorphine augmentation (added to venlafaxine XR)
canadian_sites_named: CAMH / Temerty Centre, University of Toronto ON
place_detail: 3 sites: St. Louis MO, Pittsburgh PA, Toronto ON. Site-level recruitment reported (Toronto n=36, 42%).
place_notes: Toronto enrolled the most participants (42% of total).
race_categories: White; Black; Other or multiple
race_crosswalk: White -> Not visible minority; Black -> Black; Other/multiple -> mixed
race_notes: White 87-93%. Predominantly White older adults (>=50). Tested as baseline difference (p=0.71) but not analyzed.
disability_notes: CIRS-G used but not as disability measure.
consort_equity_notes: Race, sex, age, education reported. No equity subgroup analyses. Toronto site enrolled 42%.
confidence_notes: NIMH funded. Older adults (>=50). Toronto site had highest enrollment. 87-93% White.

journal: Journal of Child and Adolescent Psychopharmacology
canadian_sites_named: US and Canada sites (Purdue Pharma Canada)
place_detail: Sites in US and Canada (2014-2015); no geographic breakdown
race_notes: No race/ethnicity reported in Table 1 (contains Gender, Age, ADHD subtype, vitals only). Industry trial with no race data.
consort_equity_notes: No race. 'Gender' reported. Adolescent industry trial. CONSORT diagram provided.
confidence_notes: Purdue Pharma Canada coded Industry. Adolescent ADHD (12-17). No race despite multisite. 'Gender' (Male/Female) likely = sex.

intervention: Mirtazapine 7.5 mg once daily at bedtime
canadian_sites_named: CHUM, Montreal QC
place_detail: CHUM Montreal; community and outpatient clinic recruitment
place_notes: Single site Montreal.
race_categories: White; Black or African American; Asian
race_crosswalk: White -> Not visible minority; Black/African American -> Black; Asian -> ambiguous
race_notes: White 90% both groups. 3 categories. US-derived labels in Canadian (Quebec) study.
consort_equity_notes: Race (3 categories) + sex AND gender distinguished (sex assigned at birth vs gender identified). FOURTH study with sex/gender distinction. CONSORT followed.
confidence_notes: Quebec Research Network on Ageing coded Other government. MIRAGE trial. FOURTH study to distinguish sex from gender (joining #28, #33, #79). Sex: assigned at birth as female/male. Gender: identified as woman/man. US-derived race categories in Quebec.

intervention: Intranasal oxytocin (24 IU Syntocinon) prior to each IPT session
comparator: Placebo (intranasal) prior to each IPT session
canadian_sites_named: Concordia University / Douglas Research Centre, Montreal QC
place_detail: Montreal; recruitment via online+print ads in free subway newspaper
place_notes: Single site Montreal. English-speaking required.
race_categories: Caucasian
race_crosswalk: Caucasian -> Not visible minority (dichotomous only)
race_notes: Caucasian % only (82% placebo, 58% oxytocin). No other groups named. Uses outdated term. Fourth weakest race reporting.
education_detail: 1-8 ordinal scale (1=Grade 6 or less to 8=completed grad/professional); mean ~5 (some college)
consort_equity_notes: Caucasian % only (no other groups). Sex, age, education, social support reported. Sex as covariate in multilevel models. English required.
confidence_notes: CIHR funded. Concordia/Douglas Montreal. Outdated 'Caucasian' term. Social support (MSPSS) — only study reporting social capital measure. English required excludes francophone Montreal.
